forage._comment_capacity_by_biome: THE HUMAN FOOD WEB, BY BIOME — the exact mirror of fauna_config.json's graze.capacity_by_biome (the ANIMAL food web), and the other half of the two-stock split (docs/plan_grazing_foundation.md §1). Human-edible biomass (seeds, nuts, tubers, fruit, shellfish, inshore fish) a tile of each biome carries at capacity; every FoodModuleTag tile is seeded a patch at its biome's value, and a biome reading 0 carries NO PATCH AT ALL. A DATA TABLE OVER THE 38 BIOMES, NOT A FORMULA: every biome must appear (validate() rejects a partial table — a missing row would silently read as an invisible zero-forage dead zone; ZERO MUST BE STATED). Per-BIOME, not per-FoodModule, deliberately: the two tables must be directly comparable tile-for-tile and must be able to DISAGREE WITHIN a module — that disagreement is the agropastoral decision. The FoodModuleTag model is untouched; the module still says what KIND of gathering a tile offers (and its seasonal_weight), this table says HOW MUCH is there. THE HEADLINE INVERSIONS, against the graze table: MixedWoodland 190 forage vs 55 graze (nuts, mast, berries under a canopy that shades out the ground cover — the flagship case); Floodplain/RiverDelta/AlluvialPlain 195-210 forage vs 130-140 graze (silt + water = CROPLAND, the farm, not the pasture); PrairieSteppe 70 forage vs 240 graze (grass: the animals feast, the humans get seed heads). Retired: the old flat carrying_capacity of 120 on every food-module tile, which made one food web spatial and the other a constant and so made 'your best farm is not your best pasture' UNTRUE BY CONSTRUCTION.
forage._comment_cultivation: The plant rung-2 BUILD dials (progress_per_turn, decay_per_turn and the old cultivating_yield_fraction) live in intensification_ladder.json -> the plant:tended rung's `build` block, and as of slice 4 so do the earned-knowledge dials (the old knowledge_progress_per_turn 0.05 / knowledge_completion_threshold 1.0 -> the ladder's `knowledge` block, same values, previously duplicated verbatim in fauna_config's husbandry). Both food webs climb — and now LEARN — on the same numbers. What remains here is the plant web's own economy.
forage._comment_navigable_river: The capacity_by_biome NavigableRiver row (130) is VESTIGIAL — a navigable hex now reads its UNDERLYING biome (resource_terrain) plus navigable_river_forage_bonus, so the row is bypassed. Left only to keep the table total.
